overview: Win quickly by deploying cheap threats and attacking every turn. The clock is the plan: race the opponent's life total down before their more powerful cards take over. Time works against you, so trade resources for tempo and damage.
win_conditions: Reduce the opponent to 0 life with repeated combat damage
Finish a stalled board with reach: burn spells or evasive attackers

keep_criteria: Two or three lands plus multiple one- and two-drop threats
A curve that lets you play a creature every turn from turn 1
At least one way to push damage through a blocker
mulligan_criteria: Zero or one lands, or five-plus lands with no early plays
Only expensive cards — nothing to do before turn 3
A hand of all situational answers and no proactive threats

examples.reason: Two lands and three one-or-two-mana creatures is a fast, functional aggro hand.
One land with no one-drop stalls the clock; aggro cannot afford a slow start.

early_game: Deploy a threat on turn 1 and keep adding to the board every turn
Attack into open mana unless the trade clearly favors the opponent
Spend removal only on blockers that stop your clock
mid_game: Keep attacking; count exact damage and plan the turn you can kill
Play around sweepers by not overcommitting once you have lethal in reach
Use evasion and combat tricks to break through stabilizing blockers
late_game: Convert remaining cards directly into damage — burn the opponent out
Avoid long games; if the board stalls, find reach rather than trading down

key_cards.name: Cheap threats
key_cards.notes: Your one- and two-drops are the game plan; protect them and keep attacking.

key_cards.name: Reach / burn
key_cards.notes: Save direct damage for the opponent's face when the board stalls.

sequencing_tips: Play your lowest-impact creature into untapped lands so removal trades down
Hold combat tricks until a profitable block or a lethal swing
Do not walk every threat into an obvious sweeper — leave a follow-up in hand

matchups.advice: Apply pressure but do not overextend into a board wipe; keep a threat in reserve.
You are the clock — race. Mulligan aggressively for the fastest possible draw.
watch_for: life payment that you can punish

common_threats: Board wipes that erase your developed board
Cheap efficient blockers that brick your attackers
